# Prune Stale WIP Revisions Using Revsets ## Background You are an open-source maintainer working on a project called `mylib` hosted on GitHub. You use Jujutsu (`jj`) as your version control system over a Git-backed repository. Over the past few days you have been experimenting locally and left behind several scratch/WIP (work-in-progress) revisions that were never promoted to a proper bookmark or given a meaningful description. You now want to clean up the repository by **abandoning** those stale WIP revisions before pushing your real work upstream. The repository already has the following history set up for you: ``` root() └── "Initial project scaffold" ← bookmark: main └── "Add core library module" ← bookmark: feature/core ├── "(no description set)" ← WIP scratch commit A (no bookmark) └── "(no description set)" ← WIP scratch commit B (no bookmark) ``` Both WIP commits are: * Direct children of the `feature/core` bookmark commit * Empty of any useful description (description is blank / empty string) * Not pointed to by any local bookmark Your task is to **abandon all mutable revisions that have an empty description AND are not ancestors of any local bookmark**, then verify the result. ## Starting Environment * Working directory: `/home/user/mylib` * The repository is already initialised as a `jj` git-backed repo * User identity is already configured in jj config * The commit history described above is already in place * `jj` binary is available in `PATH` ## Your Task Complete the following steps **inside `/home/user/mylib`**: ### Step 1 — Inspect the current log Run `jj log` to see the current history and identify the WIP revisions: ```bash cd /home/user/mylib jj log ``` You should see at least four revisions: the root, `main`, `feature/core`, and two WIP commits that have no description and no bookmark. ### Step 2 — Abandon all WIP revisions using a revset Use a single `jj abandon` command with a revset expression to abandon **all mutable revisions that have an empty description and are not ancestors of any local bookmark**. The revset you need is: ``` mutable() & empty_description & ~::bookmarks() ``` where `empty_description` is expressed using the `description("")` function (which matches commits whose description is the empty string). The exact command to run: ```bash jj abandon 'mutable() & description("") & ~::bookmarks()' ``` This revset reads as: * `mutable()` — only commits that are not immutable (i.e., not protected remote/tag heads) * `description("")` — only commits whose description is the empty string * `~::bookmarks()` — exclude commits that are ancestors-of (or equal to) any local bookmark target **Important**: The `~::bookmarks()` part uses the `::` range operator to compute the set of all ancestors of bookmark targets (inclusive), and then negates it with `~`. This ensures you only abandon commits that are genuinely not reachable from any named bookmark. ### Step 3 — Verify the result After abandoning, confirm the cleanup was successful: ```bash jj log ``` You should now see **only** the revisions that have bookmarks (`main`, `feature/core`) and the working-copy commit. The two WIP scratch commits must no longer appear in the visible history. Also verify with a revset query: ```bash jj log -r 'mutable() & description("") & ~::bookmarks()' ``` This should output **nothing** (or an empty result), confirming all matching WIP revisions have been abandoned. ## Expected Final State * The two WIP revisions (empty description, no bookmark) are **abandoned** (no longer visible) * The `main` bookmark still points to the "Initial project scaffold" commit * The `feature/core` bookmark still points to the "Add core library module" commit * `jj log -r 'mutable() & description("") & ~::bookmarks()'` returns an empty result * The file `/home/user/mylib/README.md` still exists (it was part of the scaffold) * The file `/home/user/mylib/src/lib.rs` still exists (it was part of the core module) ## Verification Conditions Automated tests will check: 1. `jj log -r 'mutable() & description("") & ~::bookmarks()'` in `/home/user/mylib` returns an empty result (exit 0 but no commits shown, or the message indicating no revisions) 2. `jj bookmark list` shows both `main` and `feature/core` bookmarks still present 3. The file `/home/user/mylib/README.md` exists 4. The file `/home/user/mylib/src/lib.rs` exists
